OPPOSITION TO PLTFS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX.REL. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE GARAMENDI, JOHN AS CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMMISSIONER

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS HARTFORD LIFE INSURANCE CO. AND HARTFORD LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. 1.) THE ABATIE DECISION DOES NOT CHANGE THE COURT'S DECISION. 2.) DEFENDANT'S ORDER WAS NOT AN UNDERGROUND REGULATION; IT ONLY APPLIED TO PLAINTIFF. 3.) THIS CASE INVOLVED ONLY LEGAL ISSUES. 4.) THE DEFINITION OF "REGULATION" IS UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; THE DEFINITION OF "STATE LAW" IS UNDER ERISA. 5.) THE COURT DID NOT MAKE FACTUAL FINDINGS. 6.) THE COURT PROPERLY REJECTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. 7.) AS A MATTER OF LAW, DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO WITHDRAW THE ENTIRE POLICY UNDER INSURANCE CODE SECTION 10291.5. 8.) THE REFERENCE TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1654 WAS NOT ERRONEOUS. MR. LEW IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER ON THE MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL AND AMENDED JUDGMENT AS TO THE RELIEF GRANTED TO HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Plaintiffs Hartford Life Insurance Company And Hartford Life And Accident Insurance Company'S Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Feb-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF HARTFORD LIFE INSURANCE CO. AND HARTFORD LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL.
APPEARANCES: PAMELA E. COGAN, ESUIRE (650.364.8200) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DAVID LEW, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (DEPT. OF JUSTICE, 510.622.2225) AND DONALD HILLA, ESQUIRE (DEPT. OF INSURANCE, 415.538.4108) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. 1.) THE ABATIE DECISION DOES NOT CHANGE THE COURT'S DECISION. 2.) DEFENDANT'S ORDER WAS NOT AN UNDERGROUND REGULATION; IT ONLY APPLIED TO PLAINTIFF. 3.) THIS CASE INVOLVED ONLY LEGAL ISSUES. 4.) THE DEFINITION OF "REGULATION" IS UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW; THE DEFINITION OF "STATE LAW" IS UNDER ERISA. 5.) THE COURT DID NOT MAKE FACTUAL FINDINGS. 6.) THE COURT PROPERLY REJECTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. 7.) AS A MATTER OF LAW, DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO WITHDRAW THE ENTIRE POLICY UNDER INSURANCE CODE SECTION 10291.5. 8.) THE REFERENCE TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 1654 WAS NOT ERRONEOUS. MR. LEW IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER ON THE MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL AND AMENDED JUDGMENT AS TO THE RELIEF GRANTED TO HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY. (302/PJM/pb) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
